channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: Trump, who until recently was chasing the Nobel Peace Prize, has launched 7 'wars'
Who is Hassan Khomeini, Ayatollah Khomeini’s grandson? Could he be Iran’s next Supreme Leader?
Will UAE, Saudi, and other Gulf States join the war as Iran strikes the region?
How Europe has been drawn into Iran war as its military bases come under attack
US embassy in Riyadh attacked, stranded Indians return from UAE… The latest as Iran war enters Day 4
FirstUp: US Congress to be briefed on Iran conflict, Canada's Carney begins Australia visit
Strategic suicide, not strategic depth: Why Pakistan’s ‘jihadi chickens’ in Afghanistan have come home to roost
item.description: As clashes flare along the Durand Line, Islamabad’s long-touted ‘strategic depth’ doctrine collapses under the weight of its own contradictions

item.title: Khamenei gone, now comes the dangerous part: Why Trump must resist regime change trap in Iran
item.description: A more rational strategy for the US would be to keep its intervention limited to neutralising Iran’s nuclear capabilities, re-establishing deterrence and reopening diplomatic channels with those in authority in Tehran post-Khamenei’s death

item.title: Is it the beginning of the end of the existing Iranian regime?
item.description: The regime appears prepared, security forces remain loyal, and ideological narratives are being activated. Yet structural exhaustion, economic pressure, leadership transition, minority alienation and generational discontent introduce vulnerabilities unseen in earlier crises

item.title: Iran at a crossroads: Why US, Israel must take correct lessons from history
item.description: Regime change from the air has historically proven elusive. There is little structural reason to assume that this case is categorically different

item.title: When Frankenstein’s monster strikes: Collapse of Pakistan’s strategic delusion
item.description: For years, Pakistan believed Afghanistan could be shaped through the Taliban from behind the curtain. Influence in Kabul was treated as insurance, a buffer against vulnerability elsewhere

item.title: The runaway ‘nagin’ of Uttar Pradesh: Decoding India’s enduring love affair with snakes
item.description: A bizarre elopement story from Uttar Pradesh reveals how deeply snake myths continue to shape the country’s cultural imagination
